documentLabel: Certificate of Good Standing (a.k.a. Certificate of Status)
summary: Delaware has no 'register extract' equivalent to a Handelsregisterauszug. The official state-issued document evidencing existence, exact registered name, incorporation date and current good standing is the Certificate of Good Standing from the Delaware Secretary of State / Division of Corporations; a certified copy of the Certificate of Incorporation with subsequent filings is also orderable. Closest official base document proving registration.

evidence.basis: Delaware Division of Corporations, official 'About Agency' page
evidence.quote: The Division of Corporations now offers online Name Reservations, Entity Status and General Information.
evidence.note: Same page lists 'Order a Good Standing Certificate' as a service.

evidence.basis: Delaware General Corporation Law, Title 8, § 106 (commencement of corporate existence)
evidence.quote: Upon the filing with the Secretary of State of the certificate of incorporation, executed and acknowledged in accordance with § 103 of this title, the incorporator or incorporators who signed the certificate ... shall, from the date of such filing, be and constitute a body corporate.
evidence.note: The charter is a filed public record establishing existence, the substrate for a good-standing certificate.

caveats: The Certificate of Good Standing evidences existence and status only; it does NOT list directors, officers, shareholders, or capital.
Delaware issues no periodic 'extract' document; equivalence to a REGISTERAUSZUG is a functional mapping, not a like-for-like document.

summary: Delaware does NOT hold or publish any list of shareholders. Ownership is recorded in the corporation's private stock ledger, administered by/for the corporation, not the state. Access is limited to stockholders exercising a statutory inspection right (proper purpose), not the public and not via the Division of Corporations. No document proving current shareholders is obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: Delaware General Corporation Law, Title 8, § 219(c) (stock ledger definition)
evidence.quote: the stock ledger shall be the only evidence as to who are the stockholders entitled by this section to examine the list required by this section or to vote in person or by proxy
evidence.note: § 219(c) defines the stock ledger as records administered by/for the corporation.

evidence.basis: Delaware General Corporation Law, Title 8, § 220(b) (inspection of books and records)
evidence.quote: the corporation's stock ledger, a list of its stockholders, and its other books and records
evidence.note: Held at the corporation, disclosed only on a qualifying stockholder demand, never filed with the state.

caveats: Shareholders are entirely private in Delaware; no state document evidences them. Ownership proof must come from the company (stock ledger, cap table) — outside the registry.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Certificate of Incorporation (the charter)
summary: For a Delaware corporation the constitutive/charter document is the Certificate of Incorporation, which IS filed with the Secretary of State and obtainable as a certified copy — the correct analog of a Gesellschaftsvertrag/Satzung. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: the bylaws are a SEPARATE internal governance document, NOT filed with the state and NOT provable from the registry. So the charter is provable; the bylaws are not.

evidence.quote: Upon the filing with the Secretary of State of the certificate of incorporation ... shall, from the date of such filing, be and constitute a body corporate.
evidence.note: The Certificate of Incorporation is a filed public instrument; certified copies orderable from the Division.

evidence.quote: The original or other bylaws of a corporation may be adopted, amended or repealed by the incorporators, by the initial directors ... or, before a corporation ... has received any payment for any of its stock, by its board of directors.
evidence.note: Bylaws are adopted internally; no statute requires filing bylaws with the state, so bylaws are not a registry document.

caveats: MAPPING CHOICE: mapped to the filed Certificate of Incorporation (the charter), which IS provable. If the KYC definition instead targets the BYLAWS, the status is not_provable_from_registry — bylaws are never filed with Delaware.
The Satzung/charter equivalent is the Certificate of Incorporation (filed); the bylaws are the internal governance document (private).

documentLabel: Certificate of Good Standing (a.k.a. Certificate of Status)
summary: As with corporations, Delaware issues no register extract for LLCs. The official state document evidencing existence, exact registered name, formation date and current good standing is the Certificate of Good Standing from the Division of Corporations; a certified copy of the Certificate of Formation with subsequent filings is also orderable. Base document proving registration.

evidence.basis: Delaware Division of Corporations, official 'About Agency' page
evidence.quote: The Division of Corporations now offers online Name Reservations, Entity Status and General Information.
evidence.note: Same page/FAQ offer 'Order a Good Standing Certificate' for entities including LLCs.

evidence.basis: Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6, § 18-201(b) (formation on filing)
evidence.quote: The certificate of formation shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State
evidence.note: The filed Certificate of Formation is the public record underlying the good-standing/status certificate.

caveats: The Certificate of Good Standing evidences existence/status only; it lists no members, managers, or capital.
No periodic 'extract' document exists; equivalence to a REGISTERAUSZUG is a functional mapping.

summary: Delaware does NOT hold any list of LLC members. The Certificate of Formation is not required to name members or managers, and membership is governed by the private limited liability company (operating) agreement, which may be written, oral or implied and is never filed. No document proving current members is obtainable from the registry.

evidence.basis: Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6, § 18-201(a) (contents of certificate of formation)
evidence.quote: Any other matters the members determine to include therein
evidence.note: Required contents are only the LLC name and registered office/agent; naming members/managers is NOT required, so the public filing does not evidence ownership.

evidence.basis: Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6, § 18-101(9) (definition of LLC agreement)
evidence.quote: "Limited liability company agreement" means any agreement (whether referred to as a limited liability company agreement, operating agreement or otherwise), written, oral or implied, of the member or members as to the affairs of a limited liability company and the conduct of its business.
evidence.note: The document defining membership is private (may even be oral/implied) and not filed with the state.

caveats: Members are entirely private in Delaware; no state document evidences them. Ownership proof must come from the company (operating agreement, member ledger) — outside the registry.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the Certificate of Formation (the charter)
summary: The constitutive/charter document filed with the state for a Delaware LLC is the Certificate of Formation, which IS filed and obtainable as a certified copy — the provable base document. CRITICAL: it is minimal (only the LLC name and registered office/agent). The document that actually functions as the Gesellschaftsvertrag in substance — the operating agreement governing members, capital and management — is PRIVATE and NOT provable from the registry.

evidence.basis: Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6, § 18-201(a)-(b)
evidence.quote: The certificate of formation shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State
evidence.note: Required contents per §18-201(a) are only the LLC name and registered office/agent; the filed charter is obtainable as a certified copy.

evidence.basis: Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6, § 18-101(9)
evidence.quote: "Limited liability company agreement" means any agreement ... written, oral or implied, of the member or members as to the affairs of a limited liability company and the conduct of its business.
evidence.note: The substantive constitution (operating agreement) is private and never filed.

caveats: MAPPING CHOICE: mapped to the filed Certificate of Formation (the charter), which IS provable, but it carries almost no substantive constitutional content — the true Gesellschaftsvertrag analog is the operating agreement, which is PRIVATE (may be oral/implied) and not_provable_from_registry.
